text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, especially in software development. Instead of planning everything at the start, teams work in short cycles called sprints. Each sprint usually lasts one to four weeks, and at the end, the team delivers a small but working part of the project. Every day, team members hold short standup meetings to discuss progress and any problems. All tasks are kept in a backlog, which is a list of work to be done. These tasks are often written as user stories, describing what a user needs from the software. This iterative development process allows teams to adapt quickly to changes.
